automatic_widget_detection.overview: This file defines how the BAW Package Manager mode automatically detects new widgets and updates the packaging configuration to include them.

step.details: Use list_files tool to get all directories in the widgets folder. Each directory that contains a widget/config.json file is a valid widget.
For each directory found, check if it contains the required widget/config.json file. This confirms it's a valid widget ready for packaging.
Read package_baw.py to get the current WIDGET_NAMES list. This shows which widgets are already configured for packaging.
Compare the list of valid widgets found in the widgets directory with the WIDGET_NAMES list in package_baw.py. Any widget not in the list is considered new and should be added.
If new widgets are found, update the WIDGET_NAMES list in package_baw.py. Add new widgets in alphabetical order to maintain consistency.
Inform the user about any new widgets that were detected and added to the packaging configuration. This provides transparency about what will be included.

widget_validation_criteria.criterion: config.json is valid JSON with required fields (name, description, etc.)
Optional: Contains {WidgetName}.svg icon file for BAW palette display

icon_detection.description: Widget palette icons are automatically detected and packaged when present. Icons enhance the user experience by providing visual identification in the BAW Process Designer palette.
icon_detection.file_naming: Icon files must be named exactly as {WidgetName}.svg and placed in the widget root directory. Example: For a widget named "ProgressBar", the icon file should be "ProgressBar.svg"

behavior.when_present: Icon is automatically packaged as a managed asset and linked to the coach view
behavior.when_missing: Widget packages successfully but appears without an icon in the BAW palette

icon_detection.note: Icon detection and packaging is fully automatic. No configuration changes are needed. See 9_widget_icons.xml for detailed icon requirements and best practices.

alphabetical_ordering.description: When adding new widgets to WIDGET_NAMES, maintain alphabetical order for consistency and easier maintenance. This makes it easier to spot missing widgets and keeps the configuration organized.

when_to_detect.trigger: User mentions creating or adding a new widget and wants to package
User explicitly asks to update the packaging configuration

reporting_format.new_widgets_found: Report: "Detected N new widget(s): [widget names]" Report: "Adding to packaging configuration..." Report: "Updated package_baw.py"
reporting_format.no_new_widgets: Report: "All widgets already configured for packaging" Report: "Proceeding with packaging..."

error.action: Skip the directory and report it as invalid
error.message: Warning: Directory 'X' does not contain a valid widget structure

error.symptom: config.json exists but is not valid JSON
error.action: Skip the widget and report the error
